ocr.text: The data presented in Table 1 offers a comparative analysis of female participants across two distinct cohorts, with a total
sample size of N = 55 and N = 57, respectively. This analysis is essential for understanding the demographic composition of the
study and the potential implications for the research outcomes.
Characteristic  Verum  Placebo
Migraine type
Previous migraine prevention
Participants with no previous preventions n (%)  40 (72.7)  36 (63.2)
Participants with 1-3 previous preventions n (%)  12 (21.8)  20 (35.0)
Participants with more than 3 previous preventions n (%)  3 (5.4)  1 (1.8)
Medical history, diseases n (%)  43 (42.1)  59 (57.8)
Concomitant medication n (%)  24 (36.3)  42 (63.6)
The column labeled "Female n (%)" highlights the representation of female participants, revealing that in the first cohort, 48

women (87.3%) were involved, while in the second cohort, this number slightly decreased to 49 women (86.0%). 

These figures
suggest a consistent and robust participation of female subjects across both groups, which is critical for assessing gender-related
variables in the context of the study. Additionally, the marginal decline in the percentage of female participants from the first
cohort to the second may warrant further exploration, particularly in relation to the study’s recruitment strategies and participant
engagement initiatives. Understanding these dynamics is vital for drawing accurate conclusions and ensuring that the findings
are representative of the wider population, thereby enhancing the validity of the research outcomes. Overall, this table provides
a foundational overview of participant demographics essential for informed interpretation of the study's results.
